Text:
Cryogenics is the branches of physics and engineering that study the production and behavior of materials at extremely low temperatures. Various gases are liquefied at different temperatures under standard atmospheric pressure. For instance, Helium boils at a extremely low temperature of 4.2 Kelvin (-268.9 degrees Celsius). Hydrogen becomes a liquid and boils at 20.28 Kelvin (-252.87 degrees Celsius). Neon, another noble gas, boils at 27.07 Kelvin (-246.08 degrees Celsius). Nitrogen, which is commonly used in laboratories, boils at 77 Kelvin (-196 degrees Celsius). Oxygen, widely used in aerospace applications, boils at 90.2 Kelvin (-182.96 degrees Celsius). Argon boils at 87.3 Kelvin (-185.8 degrees Celsius).
